payload_action: Attempted login with multiple valid credential combinations including different emails and password formats
outcome_version_a_insecure: Login works after double-clicking the signup button — unclear if this is a UI bug or a race condition on the request
outcome_version_b_secure: FAIL — Could not log in at all with any credentials tried. Tested multiple email and password combinations. Secure version appears to have a login bug blocking all authentication
notes: Secure version login is completely broken for normal users — this needs to be investigated by dev before further testing of secure version is possible. Insecure version required a double-click to trigger signup which suggests a frontend event handling issue, possibly the button submitting twice or the first click not registering.

payload_action: Attempted to change profile image via the avatar upload feature on insecure version
outcome_version_a_insecure: FAIL — Could not change the profile image at all. Feature appears broken. No image URL was accepted or displayed
outcome_version_b_secure: Not tested — secure login is blocked (see TC-001)
notes: Image upload not functional on insecure version. Cannot test SSRF vulnerability as intended because the feature itself does not work. Dev needs to investigate whether the uploads directory is configured correctly and whether the avatar URL endpoint is reachable.

payload_action: Entered an invalid/malformed email address in the profile update form and saved
outcome_version_a_insecure: EXPLOITED — Invalid email was accepted and saved to the profile with no validation. No format check on the backend at all
outcome_version_b_secure: Not tested — secure login is blocked (see TC-001)
notes: Insecure version accepts any string as an email with no server-side validation. This is expected behaviour for the insecure version and confirms the lack of input validation. Secure version should reject this via the Joi updateProfileSchema email validator — needs to be re-tested once TC-001 login bug is resolved.

target_vulnerability: A03: Injection / A01: Broken Access Control
payload_action: Attempted to look up recipient accounts using account numbers of various lengths: 7, 10, 11, and 15 digits
outcome_version_a_insecure: FAIL — Could not find any account regardless of the number length used. None of the lookup attempts returned a valid account. Could not proceed to test the transfer flow
outcome_version_b_secure: Not tested — secure login is blocked (see TC-001)
notes: Account numbers in this app are generated as 8 digits (10000000 to 99999999 range per the source code). Testing with 7, 10, 11, 15 digit numbers is correct boundary testing but a valid 8-digit account number from a registered test account is needed to confirm the lookup works at all. Recommend dev provides a seeded test account or tester registers a second account and uses that account number. Transfer IDOR and SQL injection tests are blocked until this is resolved.
